scenario.context: B2B fintech — payment processing API. Customer-facing dashboard handles payment cards (PCI scope).

log_lines.text: Server accepts: TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1, TLS 1.2, TLS 1.3
Certificate chain: Let's Encrypt → ISRG Root X1 (valid)
SPF record found: v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com include:mailgun.org -all
DMARC policy is 'none' — spoofed emails not blocked
Checking common subdomains for dangling CNAMEs...
staging.quillfinance.io → CNAME to deleted Heroku app
Testing MX servers for STARTTLS support...
BIMI: not configured (would require DMARC quarantine/reject)

Querying Have I Been Pwned domain endpoint...
Found 23 employee accounts in known breaches

reveals.html:
Quill Finance — Security Posture Report
Combined external assessment · 4 tools · demo.quillfinance.io
C+
SSL/TLS
B-
DNS Security
D+
Email Security
D
Breach Exposure
23

Top findings, sorted by severity
Critical
Subdomain takeover risk: staging.quillfinance.io
CNAME points to a deleted Heroku app. An attacker can claim the Heroku name and serve content from your subdomain. Used for phishing and OAuth bypass.
dns_security_check · CWE-1004 · staging.quillfinance.io → quill-staging-app.herokuapp.com (404)
Remove CNAME →
Critical
DMARC policy is 'none' — emails can be spoofed
Phishers can send mail as @quillfinance.io and recipients won't see it as spoofed. With 23 breached employees, this is a credible BEC vector.
email_security_audit · v=DMARC1; p=none; rua=mailto:dmarc@quillfinance.io
Update DMARC →
High
14 employee passwords exposed in breaches
Out of 23 breached employee accounts, 14 had passwords exposed. Force a password reset and confirm MFA on all of them. LinkedIn 2021 is the largest source.
credential_check · 23 accounts across 8 breaches · 14 with passwords
Force resets →
High
TLS 1.0 still enabled
PCI DSS 4.0 prohibits TLS 1.0. Disable on the load balancer. No legitimate clients still need it.
ssl_tls_audit · accepted_protocols=[1.0, 1.1, 1.2, 1.3]
Fix TLS config →
Medium
Certificate expires in 12 days
Let's Encrypt cert renewal hasn't fired. Likely an expired auto-renewal hook. Get a fresh cert deployed before Day 14.
ssl_tls_audit · CN=*.quillfinance.io · expires 2026-05-15
Renew now →
Medium
CAA records missing
Without CAA records, any CA can issue a certificate for your domain. Add CAA records for your authorized CA(s) only.
dns_security_check · suggested: 0 issue "letsencrypt.org"
Add CAA →

What your agent did

A natural-language agent prompt produces this report. Here's the call your agent made:

// Agent: "Audit demo.quillfinance.io's external posture" await mcp.call("ssl_tls_audit", { hostname: "demo.quillfinance.io" }); await mcp.call("dns_security_check", { domain: "quillfinance.io" }); await mcp.call("email_security_audit", { domain: "quillfinance.io", include_mx_analysis: true }); await mcp.call("credential_check", { check_type: "domain", target: "quillfinance.io" }); // Total cost: $1.50 (SSL $0.25 + DNS $0.25 + Email $0.50 + Breach $0.50)
